Accordion.componentDescription: Accordion displays collapsible content sections. Use it for FAQs, progressive disclosure of settings, expandable detail rows, and any scenario where showing all content at once would overwhelm the user. Supports single and multi-expand modes.
features: Two border styles: BORDER (with dividers) and NO_BORDER (seamless)
Single expand (default) or multi-expand mode via isMultiple
Rich AccordionItem trigger: leftSlot, rightSlot, subtext, subtextSlot, triggerSlot
Accordion.compositionPattern: Accordion wraps AccordionItem children. Each AccordionItem must have a unique 'value' string, a 'title', and 'children' for the expandable content. The Accordion component manages which items are open. Use defaultValue (string or string[]) for uncontrolled initial state.

props.propDescription: React element to render as children
props.llmContext: React element to render as children

props.propDescription: Controls border/divider style between accordion items
props.llmContext: BORDER adds visible borders/dividers between items, good for structured content like FAQs and forms. NO_BORDER removes separators for a seamless appearance, suitable for sidebar navigation or embedded settings panels.

props.propDescription: Initially open item value(s) in uncontrolled mode
props.llmContext: Set the initially open item(s) in uncontrolled mode. Pass a single value string for single-expand mode, or a string[] when isMultiple is true. Use to pre-expand the most relevant section.

props.propDescription: Currently open item value(s) in controlled mode
props.llmContext: The currently open item value(s) in controlled mode. Pass a string for single-expand or string[] for multi-expand. Pair with onValueChange for full control.

props.propDescription: Allows multiple items to be open at the same time
props.llmContext: Set to true to allow multiple AccordionItems to be open simultaneously. Use for independent settings sections where opening one shouldn't close others. Leave false (default) for FAQs and step-by-step flows where only one section should be active.

props.propDescription: Called with new open value(s) when expansion state changes
props.llmContext: Called with the new open value(s) when user expands or collapses an item. Update your state here. In single-expand mode, receives a string; in multi-expand mode, receives a string[].

usageExamples.description: Single-expand FAQ with bordered style.
usageExamples.code:

usageExamples.title: Multi-expand settings accordion
usageExamples.description: Settings grouped in sections, multiple can be open simultaneously.
usageExamples.code:
<Accordion
  accordionType={AccordionType.NO_BORDER}
  isMultiple
  defaultValue={['notifications', 'privacy']}
>
  <AccordionItem
    value="notifications"
    title="Notifications"
    subtext="Email, SMS, and push settings"
    leftSlot={<BellIcon />}
  >
    <NotificationSettings />
  </AccordionItem>
  <AccordionItem
    value="privacy"
    title="Privacy"
    subtext="Data sharing and visibility"
    leftSlot={<LockIcon />}
  >
    <PrivacySettings />
  </AccordionItem>
  <AccordionItem
    value="billing"
    title="Billing"
    subtext="Payment methods and invoices"
    leftSlot={<CreditCardIcon />}
    isDisabled
  >
    <BillingSettings />
  </AccordionItem>
</Accordion>

usageExamples.title: Controlled accordion
usageExamples.description: Accordion with external control over which item is expanded.
usageExamples.code:
<Accordion
  accordionType={AccordionType.BORDER}
  value={openItem}
  onValueChange={setOpenItem}
>
  <AccordionItem value="step-1" title="Step 1: Account details">
    <AccountForm onComplete={() => setOpenItem('step-2')} />
  </AccordionItem>
  <AccordionItem value="step-2" title="Step 2: Payment">
    <PaymentForm onComplete={() => setOpenItem('step-3')} />
  </AccordionItem>
  <AccordionItem value="step-3" title="Step 3: Review">
    <ReviewPanel />
  </AccordionItem>
</Accordion>

Alert.componentDescription: Alert displays important contextual messages — feedback, warnings, errors, or informational notices — inline within a page. Unlike Snackbar (ephemeral), Alert is persistent and part of the page layout. Use it for form validation summaries, system status messages, and contextual guidance.
features: Seven semantic variants: PRIMARY, SUCCESS, WARNING, ERROR, PURPLE, ORANGE, NEUTRAL
Two fill styles: SUBTLE (light tinted background) and NO_FILL (transparent, border only)
Optional primary and secondary action buttons embedded in the alert
Configurable action placement: BOTTOM (stacked under description) or RIGHT (inline with heading)
Optional close button via onClose callback
Width control via minWidth, maxWidth, width props

props.propDescription: Required. The bold title text of the alert
props.llmContext: Required. Short, direct title for the alert (e.g., 'Payment failed', 'File uploaded'). Keep it under 60 characters. This is the first thing users read, so make it immediately clear what happened.

props.propDescription: Required. The body text beneath the heading
props.llmContext: Required. Supporting text that explains the heading in more detail. Provide actionable next steps or context. For errors, explain what went wrong and how to fix it. For success, confirm what was accomplished.

props.typeDefinition: enum AlertVariant { PRIMARY = 'primary', SUCCESS = 'success', WARNING = 'warning', ERROR = 'error', PURPLE = 'purple', ORANGE = 'orange', NEUTRAL = 'neutral' }
props.propDescription: Controls color scheme and icon
props.llmContext: Choose based on the semantic meaning: ERROR for failures and validation errors; WARNING for potential problems that need attention; SUCCESS for completed actions; PRIMARY for general informational notices; NEUTRAL for low-priority context; PURPLE/ORANGE for branded or custom contexts.

props.propDescription: Controls the background fill of the alert
props.llmContext: SUBTLE (default) uses a lightly tinted background — appropriate for most alerts. NO_FILL uses only a border with no background, good when the alert sits inside a card or already-colored container and a filled background would clash.

props.propDescription: Primary action button displayed inside the alert
props.llmContext: Pass an object { label: string, onClick: () => void } for the main action button within the alert. Use for actions like 'Retry', 'Upgrade', or 'View details'. Rendered as a primary button.

props.propDescription: Secondary action button displayed inside the alert
props.llmContext: Pass an object { label: string, onClick: () => void } for a secondary action. Use for softer alternatives like 'Dismiss', 'Later', or 'Learn more'. Rendered as a secondary/ghost button.

props.propDescription: Callback fired when the close button is clicked; presence enables the close button
props.llmContext: Provide a callback to show a close (X) button in the alert. Without this prop, the alert is not dismissible. Use for alerts the user should be able to clear manually.

props.propDescription: Custom icon to display instead of the default variant icon
props.llmContext: Accepts a React node to override the default variant icon. Use this only when the default icon doesn't communicate the right meaning for your specific use case.

props.propDescription: Controls where action buttons are rendered relative to the description
props.llmContext: BOTTOM places action buttons in a new row beneath the description — use when button labels are long or when both primary and secondary actions are present. RIGHT places actions inline with the heading — use for single short-label actions in compact layouts.

props.propDescription: Text value for maxWidth
props.llmContext: Text value for maxWidth

props.propDescription: Text value for minWidth
props.llmContext: Text value for minWidth

props.propDescription: Text value for width
props.llmContext: Text value for width

Avatar.componentDescription: Avatar displays a visual representation of a user or entity — either an image, initials derived from a name, or a fallback icon. Use Avatar wherever you need to visually identify a person: comments, user lists, assignee pickers, profile headers, and notification items.

Multiple size variants for different UI contexts
Circular shape by default with optional square variant

Breadcrumb.componentDescription: Breadcrumb shows the user's current location in the application hierarchy and allows navigating back to parent pages. Use it at the top of content pages to orient users within deep navigation structures (3+ levels). Not needed on top-level pages.
features: Ordered list of path items with labels

Last item is automatically rendered as the current page (non-clickable)
Truncation for very long breadcrumb trails

Button.componentDescription: Button is the primary interactive element for triggering actions. It supports multiple visual types (primary, secondary, danger, success), sizes, and subtypes including icon-only and inline text variants. Use Button for form submissions, modal actions, confirmations, and any user-initiated action.
features: Four semantic button types: PRIMARY (main CTA), SECONDARY (secondary action), DANGER (destructive), SUCCESS (positive confirmation)
Three subtypes: DEFAULT (text + optional icons), ICON_ONLY (square icon button), INLINE (text-only, no background)
Loading state with spinner replaces content while async action is in progress
Leading and trailing icon slots for icons alongside text
buttonGroupPosition for rendering buttons as a connected group

props.typeDefinition: enum ButtonType { PRIMARY = 'primary', SECONDARY = 'secondary', DANGER = 'danger', SUCCESS = 'success' }
props.propDescription: Semantic type determining button color and hierarchy
props.llmContext: Controls the visual hierarchy and semantic meaning. Use PRIMARY for the single most important action on a screen (e.g., 'Submit', 'Save'). Use SECONDARY for alternatives and cancel actions. Use DANGER for irreversible destructive actions (e.g., 'Delete', 'Remove'). Use SUCCESS for positive confirmations (e.g., 'Approve', 'Confirm'). Never use more than one PRIMARY per action group.

props.propDescription: Controls button height and font size
props.llmContext: Use SMALL in dense UIs like tables, toolbars, and inline actions. Use MEDIUM as the default for most dialogs and forms. Use LARGE for prominent hero CTAs or full-width mobile buttons.

props.propDescription: Controls button shape and content layout
props.llmContext: DEFAULT is standard text button with optional icons. Use ICON_ONLY when you have a well-known icon and limited horizontal space (always add a tooltip for accessibility). Use INLINE for subtle text-only actions that should not draw heavy attention, like 'Learn more' or 'View details' inside a card.

props.propDescription: Text value for text
props.llmContext: Text value for text

props.propDescription: Icon rendered before the button text
props.llmContext: Accepts a React node (icon component). Place icons that clarify the action before the text, e.g., a download icon before 'Export'. Do not use both leadingIcon and trailingIcon together unless the design explicitly calls for it.

props.propDescription: Icon rendered after the button text
props.llmContext: Accepts a React node (icon component). Use a trailing icon for direction cues (e.g., arrow-right for navigation) or dropdown indicators. Prefer leadingIcon for action-clarifying icons.

props.propDescription: Prevents interaction and applies disabled visual style
props.llmContext: Use disabled when the action is not currently available (e.g., form is invalid, user lacks permissions). Prefer showing a tooltip explaining why the button is disabled rather than hiding it.

props.propDescription: Callback function for onClick
props.llmContext: Callback function for onClick

props.propDescription: Shows loading spinner and disables the button
props.llmContext: Set to true while an async operation triggered by this button is in progress. Automatically disables the button and shows a spinner. Always set this during form submission or API calls to prevent double-submission.

props.propDescription: Set to true to enable showSkeleton
props.llmContext: Set to true to enable showSkeleton

props.propDescription: Sets the skeletonVariant of the component
props.llmContext: Sets the skeletonVariant of the component

props.propDescription: Position within a button group to control border-radius styling
props.llmContext: Use this when rendering multiple buttons as a connected visual group (like a segmented control). Set to 'left' for the leftmost button, 'middle' for intermediate ones, and 'right' for the last one. This removes border-radius between adjacent buttons.

props.propDescription: Button expands to 100% of its container width
props.llmContext: Set to true to make the button stretch to fill its container. Commonly used in mobile layouts, modal footers, and stacked form actions.

props.propDescription: Sets the width of the component
props.llmContext: Sets the width of the component

props.propDescription: Sets the justifyContent of the component
props.llmContext: Sets the justifyContent of the component

props.typeDefinition: enum ButtonState { DEFAULT = 'default', HOVER = 'hover', ACTIVE = 'active', DISABLED = 'disabled' }
props.propDescription: Controls the state. Valid values: ButtonState.DEFAULT, ButtonState.HOVER, ButtonState.ACTIVE, ButtonState.DISABLED
props.llmContext: Controls the state. Valid values: ButtonState.DEFAULT, ButtonState.HOVER, ButtonState.ACTIVE, ButtonState.DISABLED

Modal.componentDescription: Modal is a dialog overlay that captures user focus for important interactions — confirmations, forms, detail views, and multi-step flows. It blocks the underlying page and must be explicitly dismissed. For mobile-friendly layouts, use useDrawerOnMobile to automatically switch to a Drawer on small screens.
features: Controlled open/close state via isOpen and onClose
Standard header (title + subtitle) and footer (primaryAction + secondaryAction) with show/hide toggles
Fully custom header and footer slots for non-standard layouts
headerRightSlot for adding actions or badges in the header without replacing it entirely
Optional divider between header and body content
Responsive: switches to bottom-sheet Drawer on mobile when useDrawerOnMobile is true
Skeleton loading mode while content is being fetched
Width control via minWidth, maxWidth; height via maxHeight
Backdrop click to close (enabled by default, disable with closeOnBackdropClick=false)
Modal.compositionPattern: Pass content as children between the Modal tags. For simple confirmation dialogs, use title + primaryAction + secondaryAction props. For complex forms or custom layouts, use isCustom=true with customHeader and customFooter slots.

props.llmContext: Set to true to suppress the default header/footer rendering entirely and take full control of the modal content via children. Use for non-standard layouts like image galleries, multi-panel views, or fully custom forms. When isCustom is true, title, subtitle, primaryAction, and secondaryAction props are ignored.

props.propDescription: Required. Callback fired on close button click or Escape key
props.llmContext: Required. Called when the user clicks the close button or presses Escape. Set isOpen to false inside this handler. If you need to prevent accidental dismissal (e.g., unsaved form), also set closeOnBackdropClick=false and handle confirmation logic here.

props.llmContext: Use 'primary' for general progress. Use 'success' when the process is complete or going well. Use 'warning' for processes approaching limits (e.g., 75% storage used). Use 'error' for processes at risk or over limit.

Radio.componentDescription: Radio and RadioGroup provide a mutually exclusive selection pattern. Use when the user must pick exactly one option from a small set (2–6 options). For larger sets or searchable options, use SingleSelect instead. RadioGroup is the required parent that manages the selected value and shared name.
features: Controlled (value + onChange) and uncontrolled (defaultValue) modes at the group level
Error state propagation from RadioGroup to child Radios
slot prop for supplementary React node content (icons, badges)
Radio.compositionPattern: Always wrap Radio components inside a RadioGroup. RadioGroup manages the selected value via value/defaultValue and onChange. Each Radio must have a unique value prop. The name prop on RadioGroup is required for form accessibility.

SingleSelect.componentDescription: SingleSelect is the standard dropdown for selecting one value from a list. Use it for form fields like country, category, status, or any enumerated single-choice. For multi-choice, use MultiSelect. For simple navigation menus, use Menu. SingleSelect supports search, virtualization for large lists, sub-menus, and mobile drawer mode.
features: Two variants: CONTAINER (bordered trigger) and NO_CONTAINER (minimal trigger)
Grouped items with optional group labels and separators
Virtualized rendering for large item lists
Infinite scroll with onEndReached and hasMore
SingleSelect.compositionPattern: Items are passed as SelectMenuGroupType[]. Each group has an optional groupLabel and an items array of SelectMenuItemType. If you don't need groups, pass a single group without a groupLabel. Each item needs at minimum label and value strings.

StatCard.componentDescription: StatCard displays a KPI or metric with an optional trend indicator and chart visualization. Use it in dashboards for key business metrics — revenue, user counts, conversion rates, and other numeric KPIs.
features: Four visualization variants: LINE (sparkline chart), PROGRESS_BAR (completion bar), BAR (bar chart), NUMBER (metric only)
Change indicator showing increase/decrease with percentage or value
Chart data slot for sparkline and bar variants

props.propDescription: Required. Metric title/label
props.llmContext: Required. Short metric name displayed above the value. Use concise nouns or noun phrases (e.g., 'Monthly Revenue', 'Active Users', 'Conversion Rate'). Keep under 30 characters.

props.propDescription: Required. The primary metric value (pre-formatted string)
props.llmContext: Required. The primary metric value displayed prominently. Pass pre-formatted strings (e.g., '$124,500', '82%', '24.8K'). Format numbers appropriately for the metric type before passing.

props.propDescription: React element to render as valueTooltip
props.llmContext: React element to render as valueTooltip

props.propDescription: Trend change indicator with value and direction
props.llmContext: Object { value: number, type: 'INCREASE' | 'DECREASE' } showing the trend. Use INCREASE for positive direction (green) and DECREASE for negative (red). Note that for some metrics (like 'Error Rate'), a decrease is actually positive — consider your framing.

props.propDescription: Text value for subtitle
props.llmContext: Text value for subtitle

props.typeDefinition: enum StatCardVariant { LINE = 'line', PROGRESS_BAR = 'progress', BAR = 'bar', NUMBER = 'number' }
props.propDescription: Chart visualization variant
props.llmContext: LINE adds a sparkline trend chart — use for time-series metrics like revenue or traffic. PROGRESS_BAR shows goal completion — use with progressValue for percentage-based targets. BAR adds a bar chart — use for comparing recent periods. NUMBER shows only the metric — use for simple counts or derived values where a chart would not add meaningful context.

props.propDescription: Data points for LINE or BAR chart visualization
props.llmContext: Array of data points for LINE and BAR variants. Each point should have a value field and a label/category field. Keep to 5-12 data points for readable sparklines.

props.propDescription: 0–100 completion value for PROGRESS_BAR variant
props.llmContext: Required when variant is PROGRESS_BAR. A number 0–100 representing the completion percentage. This drives the visual bar fill, while value is the display text above.

slot prop for supplementary React node content

Tabs.componentDescription: Tabs organizes content into multiple panels where only one panel is visible at a time. Use Tabs for switching between distinct views of the same resource (e.g., Overview / Settings / Analytics). It supports four visual variants and integrates with Radix UI for accessible keyboard navigation.
features: Four visual variants: BOXED (card-style), FLOATING (elevated tabs), UNDERLINE (minimal, bottom border active indicator), PILLS (rounded pill-shaped tabs)
Rich tab items: each can have left/right icon slots, new-item badge, and disabled state
Controlled and uncontrolled modes via value/onValueChange or defaultValue
Optional add-tab button and close-tab callbacks for dynamic tab management
Overflow dropdown for tabs that don't fit in the available width
Sticky header with configurable offsetTop for scrollable tab panels
expanded prop to fill the full container width
Tabs.compositionPattern: Pass all tab definitions as the items array. Each item has a value (unique key), label (display text), and content (ReactNode for the tab panel). The Tabs component renders both the tab strip and the panel — you do not need to manually conditionally render panels.

props.typeDefinition: enum TabsVariant { BOXED = 'boxed', FLOATING = 'floating', UNDERLINE = 'underline', PILLS = 'pills' }
props.propDescription: Visual style of the tab strip
props.llmContext: UNDERLINE is the most common — clean and minimal, best for page-level sections. BOXED gives a card-like appearance, good for settings or dashboard widgets. FLOATING adds elevation/shadow to the active tab, suitable for prominent navigation. PILLS uses rounded shapes, good for filter-style tabs or compact UIs.

props.propDescription: Controls the size. Valid values: TabsSize.MD, TabsSize.LG
props.llmContext: Controls the size. Valid values: TabsSize.MD, TabsSize.LG

props.propDescription: Tabs fill the full container width
props.llmContext: Set to true to make tabs stretch to fill the full width of their container, distributing tab widths evenly. Use in full-width layouts where you don't want tabs to be left-aligned.

props.propDescription: Set to true to enable fitContent
props.llmContext: Set to true to enable fitContent

props.propDescription: Array of tab definitions including labels, content, and optional slots
props.llmContext: Required. Array of TabItem objects. Each must have: value (unique string key used for controlled state), label (display text), and content (ReactNode rendered when the tab is active). Optional: leftSlot/rightSlot (React nodes for icons or badges), disable (boolean), newItem (boolean to show a 'new' badge).

props.propDescription: Called with tab value when user clicks the tab close button
props.llmContext: Callback fired with the tab value when a tab's close button is clicked. Presence of this prop enables close buttons on all non-disabled tabs. Update the items array in this callback to actually remove the tab.

props.propDescription: Callback function for onTabAdd
props.llmContext: Callback function for onTabAdd

props.propDescription: Shows overflow dropdown when tabs overflow the container
props.llmContext: Set to true to show an overflow dropdown for tabs that don't fit in the available horizontal space. Use when the number of tabs is dynamic or could be large.

props.propDescription: Displays a button to add new tabs
props.llmContext: Set to true to display a '+' button at the end of the tab strip for adding new tabs. Must also provide onTabAdd callback. Use for workspaces or editors where users can create new tabs.

props.propDescription: Text value for dropdownTooltip
props.llmContext: Text value for dropdownTooltip

props.propDescription: Text value for addButtonTooltip
props.llmContext: Text value for addButtonTooltip

props.llmContext: Set to true to enable disable

props.llmContext: Set to true to enable showSkeleton

props.llmContext: Sets the skeletonVariant of the component

props.propDescription: Tab strip sticks to top of viewport while scrolling
props.llmContext: Set to true to make the tab strip stick to the top of the viewport when scrolling through a long tab panel. Use in pages with long scrollable content inside tabs. Pair with offsetTop to account for a fixed topbar.

Tooltip.componentDescription: Tooltip shows a brief informational label on hover or focus. Use it to explain icon buttons, clarify truncated text, show keyboard shortcuts, or provide additional context for disabled elements. Tooltips should be short (under 60 characters) — use Popover for richer content.
features: Side offset for spacing from trigger

Tooltip.compositionPattern: Wrap the trigger element with Tooltip. The trigger must be a focusable element (button, link, or a div with tabIndex). Pass the tooltip text to the content prop.

props.propDescription: Required. Trigger element
props.llmContext: Required. The element that triggers the tooltip on hover/focus. Must be a focusable element. If wrapping a disabled button (which can't receive events), wrap the button in a <span> to allow the tooltip to still work.

props.llmContext: Required. The tooltip text or React node. Keep text under 60 characters for hover tooltips. For longer content or interactive elements (links, buttons), use Popover instead.

props.llmContext: Preferred side of the trigger to display the tooltip. 'top' is default and most common. Use 'bottom' when the trigger is near the top of the viewport. Use 'left' or 'right' for tooltips on list items or side-positioned elements. The tooltip will auto-flip if there's not enough space.

props.propDescription: Alignment of tooltip relative to trigger
props.llmContext: 'start', 'center', or 'end' alignment relative to the trigger. 'center' is default. Use 'start' or 'end' when the tooltip text would be clipped at viewport edges.
